feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Build
feed.subtitle: SAP Build Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: how SAP Build user license consumtion works
entry.content:
Dear community,

Last one month I am trying to identify how SAP build user license consumption works.

Here is my latest subscriptions. Earlier i subscribed SAP Build Process automation with Build-default as it is a prerequisites for joule skills availability for building skills in joule studio.

The reason why removed SAP BPA build-default subscription is that SAP started charging abnormal SAP build user ( developer ) license cost belong to SKU - 8019311 as shown below for which i never subscribed. After continuous follow up for almost a month i could recover the credits back but it impacted our development since SAP didn't explained entirely how they calculated the consumption and without their proper explanation we don't want to end up in the same situation so dropped the subscription and went ahead with free subscription there by sacrificing joule skills testing.  

https://help.sap.com/docs/build-service/build-service-guide/service-plans-and-metering

 

As i mentioned above when SAP BPA subscription is build-default and SAP build code - free , SAP BAS - free then how the SAP build user (developer ) license will work out. If SAP calculates the SAP Build charges based on SAP BPA build-default plan then there is no proper documentation where it should mention that go with only SAP BPA as it brings all the functionality of SAP build with it start building you apps and skills and we will start charging for it and no need of other subscriptions.

Please go through it and provide me your observations.

Thanks and Regards

entry.title: How to: Use OpenText Core Archive for Document Storage/Archiving from SAP BTP / Build?
entry.content:
Hello experts,

We are designing a solution on SAP BTP using SAP Build / CAP and SAP Build Process Automation and would like to use OpenText Core Archive for SAP Solutions as our document storage/archiving platform – without involving an SAP ECC/S/4HANA backend for the document upload.

Scenario:

  • We have a SAP Fiori / CAP front-end application with a file upload (PDF) functionality deployed in Cloud Foundry / BTP.
  • Uploaded PDFs should:
    1. Be stored/archived centrally in OpenText Core Archive for SAP Solutions.
    2. Be retrievable later in SAP Build Process Automation to show the document (PDF) in an approval form for review/approval.
  • We want to call OpenText directly from BTP, not via ECC/S/4HANA, and then access those documents via APIs from workflows in SAP Build Process Automation.

Questions:

  • OpenText Core Archive APIs from BTP

    • Does OpenText Core Archive for SAP Solutions expose REST APIs that can be called directly from BTP (CAP/Node/Java) for:
      • Creating (uploading) documents (PDF) with metadata?
      • Retrieving documents (either as binary or via a time-limited URL)?
  • Or is there a more “standard” pattern for using Core Archive as a central document store from BTP without ECC/S/4?
  • Are there any official guidelines or recommendations from SAP/OpenText for this type of BTP ↔ Core Archive integration?
  • Even if examples are for Extended ECM or other OpenText products, any patterns applicable to Core Archive would be helpful.

Any inputs, experiences, example architectures, or links to documentation/blogs are highly appreciated.

Thanks! in Advance.

BR,
Rakesh

entry.title: SBPA Destination Error: Destination not found.
entry.content:
I am following below tutorial from developer.sap.com and getting destination connectivity issue.
https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/build-apps-workflow-trigger.html

{error: "Error: Destination not found.↵", message: "Destination not found."}
error: "Error: Destination not found.\n" 
message: "Destination not found."

Steps

1. I have completed Create a Sales Order Process Using SAP Build Process Automation Successfully.

2. After that I have created Create a Sales Order App Using SAP Build Apps from SAP Build Successfully.

3. When I try to access from Build App Preview getting above destination issue.

4. As per tutorial it will work like this.

Regards,

Abderrahmane Kermiche

entry.title: Trigger SAP Build Process Automation Workflow from SAP Build Apps (Form & API Based)
entry.content:
Trigger SAP Build Process Automation Workflow from SAP Build Apps (Form & API Based)

When building applications using SAP Build Apps, a common requirement is to trigger workflows created in SAP Build Process Automation (BPA).

However, many developers face confusion:

Can we trigger a Form-based process directly from Build Apps?

👉 Short answer: No — Build Apps can only trigger workflows via API.
But don’t worry — there’s a proper workaround.

In this blog, we’ll cover:

  • ✅ Triggering API-based workflows
  • ✅ Triggering Form-based workflows via API workaround
  • ✅ End-to-end configuration

Understanding the Problem

In SAP Build Process Automation:

Trigger TypeCan Build Apps Trigger Directly?
Form TriggerNo
API TriggerYes

Reason:
Build Apps communicates via REST APIs, not UI forms.

ℹ️To Integrate Build Apps with Process Automation we need BTP Destination
Create Destination in SAP BTP with the same Name "sap_process_automation_service_user_access". Authentication can be either OAuth2ClientCredentials or OAuth2JWTBearer but add the correct Client ID and Client Secret ID from Service key from the instance of SAP Build Process Automation. Additional Properties are important to get the success integration. Finally save and check the connection.

Approach 1: API-Based Workflow Trigger 

Step 1: Create Process with API Trigger

  • Open your BPA project
  • Create a Process
  • Select Start Event → API Trigger

    Define Input Schema:
    {
    "FirstName": "string"
    }

Step 2: Deploy the Process

Click the three dots in SAP lobby of deployed process and publish to library. So that only you can integrate deployed process in SAP Build Apps.

After deployment, you will get:

  • Workflow API endpoint
  • Process Definition ID

Step 3: Configure SAP Build Apps

In SAP Build Apps:

1.Go to App Settings

Click Enable Authentication, Choose SAP BTP Authentication. Click Ok on the Popup.
If already SAP BTP Authentication added in App Setting Leave the Above Step

2.Integrate Workflow Process

Go to Integrations Tab Click Add Integration Button, Choose SAP Build Library. Under Processes you can select the published workflow process and  Enable process

3.Create UI

  1. Input fields:
    -> FirstName
  2. Start Process Button
    Add Logic to the above Button.

In Logic window, Add Trigger Process, choose the enabled Process and configure the input parameter as object with properties. To show status you can add either Alert/ Toast Message.
     

✅ Result

  • User give inputs in Build Apps
  • Clicks Submit
  • Workflow starts in BPA

Approach 2: Form-Based Process 

⚠️ Important Concept

Even if your process uses a Form Start Event,
👉 there is NO direct API to submit that form

So this will NOT work:
❌ Build Apps → Submit BPA Form directly

✅ Correct Workaround

🔄Convert Form Input → API Payload in SAP Build Apps

Step 1: Create Form Based Trigger Workflow in SAP Build Process Automation.

    • Here I took Leave Approval Application Workflow and deploy it. Trigger the process once then you can see the definition Id in Monitoring tab for this triggered workflow. Copy that ID for later use.
        

Step 2 : Create Build Apps Application 

  • Add SAP BTP Authentication in App Settings if not you not done yet.
  • Go to Integration tab and create a data entity by selecting SAP BTP Destination Rest API integration.
    • In Base, Choose the destination and add resource schema. ⚠️ Do not add common request header for token passing

    • Click on create and Enable it.
      • Set the relative path and query - Add static value "/public/workflow/rest/v1/workflow-instances".
      • Set Request Method as POST. 
      • Add Request Headers as List -> Key : Content-Type, Value :  application/json.
      • Add Request Body Mapper by wrap the data as json. and Save the data entity.
        ENCODE_JSON({"definitionId":"<value of the definitionId which is copied from Monitoring tab in SAP BPA>", "context": {<all parameters present in startEvent of input schema (you can refer in monitoring tab of the triggered process>}})


  • Create UI with required input fields. Add Button and configure the process flow in logic canvas.
    • Add create record and configure the resource which is create by following above steps. Set the records as object with properties. Add Toast Message/ Alert for your purpose.
    • Test the App, you will get the result and also you can cross check with monitoring tab in SAP BPA for the triggered process

⚠️ Common Issues

❌ Invalid Token 

  • Wrong OAuth configuration
  • No proper Addition Properties

❌ 401 Unauthorized

  • Client ID / Secret issue

❌ Workflow Not Starting

  • Wrong definitionID
  • Incorrect payload format

These can resolve by adding correct input configuration and Correct destination configuration.

🔄 Architecture Overview

SAP Build Apps (UI Form) ➡️ REST API Call (OAuth) ➡️ SAP Build Process Automation ➡️ Workflow Execution
 
🎯 Best Practices
✔ Validate payload before sending
✔ Use destinations for secure connectivity
✔ Always use inspect to check the console for error

Conclusion

🎉 And that’s a wrap!
You’ve now unlocked the power of triggering workflows in SAP Build Process Automation directly from SAP Build Apps using API-driven integration.
What started as a simple requirement—triggering a workflow—has evolved into a powerful architectural pattern:

  • 🎯 Build Apps handling the user experience
  • ⚙️ SAP BPA orchestrating the process logic
  • 🔗 APIs acting as the bridge between them

By moving beyond Form Triggers and embracing an API-first approach, you’re not just solving a limitation—you’re designing a solution that is scalable, flexible, and enterprise-ready.

💡 Imagine the possibilities:
From employee requests and approvals to complex cross-system automations—this pattern can power countless real-world scenarios with efficiency and control.

🚀 And this is just the beginning.
You can take it further by integrating additional services, enhancing validations, or building intelligent, event-driven workflows across your SAP landscape.

If you found this helpful or have ideas to expand this approach, feel free to connect and share your thoughts!

Let’s build smarter automation together.

LinkedIn : https://www.linkedin.com/in/abirami-t/

entry.title: Build Work Zone: No Content Items Created from S/4HANA FLP Content Exposure (0/0)
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are currently integrating SAP Build Work Zone (Standard Edition) with an SAP S/4HANA on‑premise system using Launchpad Content Exposure to SAP BTP (Exposure Version 2).

Although the content exposure in S/4HANA seems to be set up correctly, no content items are created in Build Work Zone after channel synchronization.

Observed Behavior

  • The FLP Content Exposure runs successfully in S/4HANA
  • JSON content is generated for:
    • Business Apps
    • Catalogs
    • Groups
    • Roles
  • In Build Work Zone → Channel Manager, the channel status shows Updated
  • However, the Channel Report shows:

    Content Items: 0 / 0
    No content items were created or updated

Expected Behavior

  • Catalogs, apps, and groups exposed from S/4HANA should be converted into content items in Build Work Zone and become available for site usage.

Configuration Details

  • S/4HANA FLP content includes:
    • Business apps (homepage, search)
    • Catalogs assigned to roles
    • Role created specifically for Build Work Zone consumption
  • Content exposure uses Exposure Version 2
  • Channel is created in Build Work Zone in automatic mode
  • Cloud connector and destination setup is in place

Checks Already Done

  • Verified that FLP catalogs are assigned to the role
  • Verified that the role is included in content exposure
  • Channel synchronization completes without errors
  • No errors in BWZ logs, only empty content result

Questions

  1. Which technical roles are mandatory for the S/4HANA user (or technical integration user) to allow Build Work Zone to read FLP content?
  2. Is any additional role required for content item generation?
  3. Are there additional authorization objects (e.g. /UI2/FLP_CONT) that are required?
  4. Are there known prerequisites or limitations when using Exposure Version 2 with Build Work Zone?
  5. Any checks or logs you would recommend to identify why content items are not being generated?

Any guidance or pointers would be highly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your support.

Please find the screenshots below for your reference.

Kind regards,
Om Prakash Jha

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation: My Inbox stuck in loading
entry.content:
Good day,

There seems to be an issue currently with the My Inbox in SAP Build/SAP Build Process Automation. We are receiving an error 403 when viewing the My Inbox page. Our destinations are properly defined, and have not made any changes since Friday. It still persists up to this day. 

Anyone also experiencing this and have applied a fix?

 

Thank you very much!

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation - My Inbox is stuck at loading
entry.content:
Hello,

I am currently facing an issue with My Inbox page of SAP Build Process Automation. It is currently not loading the tasks that are triggered in BPA and is just stuck in loading. Is there any fix for this?

Thank you very much!

entry.title: Send Email Activity in SAP iRPA giving error
entry.content:
I was sending email via send email activity, it was working since last 6 months. Today it showed an error- SAP irpa core error - Failed to send email Outlook does not recognize one or more names. I can't understand what's wrong here.
Can someone please help me!!!!

entry.title: Deployment Error In SAP Build Process Automation // Deployment request timeout
entry.content:
Hi,

We are facing recurring errors when deploying SBPA projects. Everything was working fine until now, but since today it has become impossible to deploy our projects: we keep having a "Deployment request timeout" error.

 

It seems like the issues reported here or here coming from SAP's side is happening again...

Thanks for your feedback.

BR,

LM.

entry.title: Exporting SAP Build Apps: Converting "Web and Mobile" to "Web Only" for Build-Default Plan
entry.content:
Hello,

I am moving my development work between tenants. My original project was created as a "Web and Mobile Application" in a tenant with a Free Plan. My new tenant is on the Build-Default plan.

When attempting to import my project, I am blocked by the issue described in KBA 3693320, as the Build-Default plan does not support "Web and Mobile" project types.

Since there is no "Convert to Web-Only" button in the UI, I am looking for the most efficient way to take my work with me.

I would like to know:

  1. Is there any way to export the project so that the target tenant accepts it as a "Web-Only" app?

  2. If direct import is impossible, is there a supported (or community-tested) way to copy objects, logic, or pages from the "Web and Mobile" project and paste them into a fresh "Web Only" project shell?

Thank you for your help!

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation: Stable selection of dynamic dropdown items in SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am building an automation to handle the "Anonymise Candidate Profiles" process in SAP SuccessFactors Admin Centre. I need to search for a candidate and select the correct one from a dropdown by matching the Email ID from an Excel sheet.

Current Problem: My current logic using "Keystroke Down" is unstable; it frequently skips items or fails to update the selection. Even when using a Collection (LI) with a For Each loop, the UI does not consistently register the click, and the automation sometimes retrieves technical metadata (JSON) instead of the visible text.

Technical Details:

  • Target Element: Li captured as a collection (Class: "fd list item").

  • Logic: Iterate through the collection -> Get text -> Compare with Excel Email -> Click matching index.

  • Challenge: Standard click/keystroke activities are not triggering the SuccessFactors selection event reliably.

Question: How can I achieve a stable selection in this specific UI? Is there a recommended Custom Script or a specific UI5/web event I should trigger to ensure the dropdown value is selected and the screen refreshes for the next candidate?

Attached are my recognition criteria and flow screenshots.

Any help is appreciated!

Thanks,

Narenthiran Annamalai.

entry.title: Error Destination, related to SAP Automation, Joule Skill and Action
entry.content:
-Update 15.05.2026-

// Already Solved. Sorry there's different type when i maintain variable Destination in Action Automation. I notice this when opening JS Script in C:\Users\{user}\AppData\Local\SAP\Intelligent RPA\Projects\{automation_id}\automations

Hi everyone, hope you are all having a great day!

I am currently running into an issue with a destination configuration in SAP Build Process Automation and would appreciate any guidance.

My Scenario: I have built a RAP application and I am using the exposed API to trigger a "Create" method via SAP Build.

What is Working: The API is functioning perfectly outside of the automation. I have successfully tested and triggered it using:

  • Postman

  • SAP Build "Actions"

  • Joule Skills

The Issue: The problem occurs when I try to execute this same step inside an Automation. It fails and throws the following error log:

"The provided destination does not follow the expected structure. Please contact support"

Has anyone encountered this specific destination error when transitioning an API call from an Action to an Automation? Are there additional destination properties or formatting rules specifically required for Automations that I might be missing?

Thanks in advance for your help

{
	"type": "automationActivity",
	"name": "irpa_core.log",
	"label": "Add new entity to PRODUCT_HEADER",
	"inputs": [
		"The provided destination does not follow the expected structure. Please contact support.",
		{
			"key": "Error",
			"value": 1
		},
		null,
		true
	],
	"containerInstanceUid": "...",
	"parentInstanceUid": "...",
	"stepId": "4",
	"line": 8,
	"file": "triggerAPI.js"
}

Here my destination, related properties already being maintain

Testing in Action on SAP Build

Testing in Joule Skill

Testing in SAP Automation

entry.title: SAP BUILD - QUERY
entry.content: Is there any sap document for best practices for sap build and sap build products ?

entry.title: What is the Use of Private Environment in SAP Build Process Automation Control Tower?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am curious to understand the purpose of the Private Environment option in SAP Build Process Automation Control Tower.

Currently, we have:

  • Shared Environment
  • Public Environment
  • Private Environment if user activate it

These environments are available while deploying processes. However, when I try to deploy a process, I do not see an option to select the Private Environment.

Because of this, I am confused about the actual use case of the Private Environment in SAP BPA Control Tower.

My questions are:

  1. What is the purpose of a Private Environment in SAP BPA?
  2. In which scenarios is it used?
  3. Why is it not available as a deployment option while deploying processes?
  4. Is it related to agent-based execution, runtime isolation, or tenant-level configuration?

It would be helpful if someone could explain the architecture or practical usage of Private Environments in real projects.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Free Edition Migration Question for Existing App
entry.content:
Hi,

I currently have a production app built using SAP Build Apps Free Edition.

I understand that the Free Edition will be deprecated on September 30, 2026 and that SAP Build is the recommended path forward.

I have located the project export feature which creates a .zip.gpg file.

Could you please clarify:

  1. Can Free Edition projects be migrated into SAP Build using these exports?
  2. If so, will the app pages, logic, data resources and project structure be retained?
  3. Is there an SAP Build option that allows me to continue maintaining my existing application in a visual low-code environment?
  4. Or would my application need to be converted to React/SAPUI5 code or rebuilt?

I’m trying to determine the best path forward and whether I can continue maintaining my existing application after the Free Edition is retired.

Thank you.

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation – Click Dynamic "Clear" Button Based on Journal Entry
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am developing a SAP Build Process Automation BOT for the Post Incoming Payments Fiori application.

The Open AP/AR Items table contains multiple rows, each with a Journal Entry value and a corresponding Clear button.

My requirement is to find a specific Journal Entry Number (e.g., 40000005) and click the Clear button in the same row.

Since the table rows are generated dynamically in SAP Fiori, I would like to know the recommended approach in SAP BPA to:

Identify a row based on a column value (Journal Entry).
Click a button in the corresponding row.
Handle dynamically generated SAPUI5 table elements.

Screenshot attached for reference.

Any guidance or examples would be appreciated.

Thanks.

entry.title: Are SAP BTP, SAP Build, Generative AI Hub and SAP HANA Cloud restricted to RISE with SAP customers,
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'd like to clarify the licensing eligibility for several SAP cloud products and would appreciate input from the community (and ideally from SAP colleagues with a licensing background).

Context: We are advising a customer who currently runs SAP S/4HANA on-premise (no RISE with SAP contract, no S/4HANA Cloud Private/Public Edition). The customer is interested in adopting the following services:

  • SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)
  • SAP Build (Apps / Process Automation / Work Zone)
  • SAP Generative AI Hub (within SAP AI Core)
  • SAP HANA Cloud

My questions:

  1. Are any of these products contractually restricted to RISE with SAP customers, or can a pure on-premise customer subscribe to them directly (e.g., via CPEA, Pay-As-You-Go, or Subscription model)?
  2. Which capabilities are only included as part of the RISE with SAP Premium Plus package, and which can be purchased standalone regardless of the ERP deployment model?
  3. Are there any technical prerequisites or limitations (e.g., required S/4HANA Cloud edition, specific connectivity setup) when consuming Generative AI Hub or HANA Cloud from an on-premise S/4HANA landscape?
  4. Does the answer differ between commercial entitlement (what you can buy) and functional integration scope (what you can actually connect to your on-premise system)?

Any pointers to official SAP documentation, product availability matrices, or licensing guides would be highly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Joule skill- Error EL1008E: Property or field 'result' cannot be found on object of type 'Map'
entry.content:
Dear All,

I am facing this issue where I am getting the error while testing the Joule skill-
EL1008E: Property or field 'result' cannot be found on object of type 'Map' - maybe not public or not valid?

The skill tester is returning null values but the same when tested from postman works fine. Not sure what is the issue here. Could you please point me to the right direction. 


1.  Test Results

a.

b. API call status

c. Output fields are null

3. Joule skill

Input parameters

Output  parameters

4. The same API is working fine when tested from postman.

Thanks and Regards,
Rudra
Joule Studio, classic edition Joule SAP Build Process Automation

entry.title: Problem in extending V4 RAP service to List Report UI via CAPM Layer
entry.content:
I have created a Managed scenario in RAP and used V4 service binding. Then I created a CAP project in BAS consuming that V4 service and metadata. In service.cds and service.js too, I didn't added any custom functionality, just basic entity projection in service.cds and basic tx function for all methods (CREATE, READ, UPDATE and DELETE) in service.js as I want to use the CRUD functionalities offered by Managed RAP scenario directly. 

Then I created a List Report Application using Local CAP Project and consuming this CAP project mentioned above. 

Now when I am previewing this application, no data is showing in the List Report by clicking GO button. If I click on Create Button, Object page is always in busy state and never loads. But If I load the Fiori Preview in RAP, all CRUD operations works fine. 

In the Console, these errors come while loading the site from cds watch in BAS - 

Can anyone help what else should be done and where? And CAP layer is required for my Requirement as I have to implement roles and securities and other security related stuff. Directly coonecting FIori App to local system and consuming service is not a feasible solution for me

entry.title: SAP Build Code BAS -/ui5-create-app fails with "@ui5/mcp-server" error and Accept Application button
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am trying to create a freestyle SAPUI5 application in SAP Build Code (BAS) using the new Joule command:
/ui5-create-app

Prompt used

/ui5-create-app Create a freestyle SAPUI5 application for the Manage Sales Order CAP service.
Use the OData V4 service:
/service/manage_Sales_Order_App

CAP Project Information
I already have a CAP project generated successfully using Joule.
Project structure:
db/
srv/
app/
The OData service is available and generated correctly.

Joule Response
Joule returns the following proposal:
Let's configure your Manage Sales Order app using JavaScript instead of TypeScript.
Namespace: com.myorg.managesalesorder
Base Path:
/home/user/projects/Manage_Sales_Order_App/app
Create App Directory: true
OData V4 URL:
service/manage_Sales_Order_App/
OData Entity Set:
SalesOrders
TypeScript: false
Once you're ready, you can accept the application proposal
by pressing the "Accept Application" button.
Issue
The "Accept Application" button never appears.

At the same time, I can see the following error in the BAS Error panel:
Failed to generate app proposal:

Cannot find package '@ui5/mcp-server'
imported from

/extbin/local/openvscode-server/extensions/
sap-ux-help-extension-1.28.0/dist/extension-min.cjs

Did you mean to import
"@ui5/mcp-server/lib/api.js"?

Source:
SAP Fiori Tools - Guided Development

Troubleshooting Performed
I have already tried:

Refreshing BAS browser window
Opening a new Joule conversation
Using a simplified /ui5-create-app prompt
Creating a new Development Space
Reopening the project

The same error occurs every time.

Environment
SAP Build Code / SAP Business Application Studio
SAP Fiori Tools - Guided Development
Extension version reported in error:
sap-ux-help-extension-1.28.0
Questions
Is this a known issue with the SAP Fiori Tools / UI5 Create Application assistant?
Has anyone encountered the missing package error:
@ui5/mcp-server
Is there any workaround available until the issue is fixed?
Any guidance would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!
